commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# lapsdumper -h
 LAPS Dumper - Running at 06-17-2026 11:05:34
 usage: lapsdumper [-h] -u USERNAME -p PASSWORD [-l LDAPSERVER] -d DOMAIN
                 [-c COMPUTER] [-o OUTPUT]
 
 Dump LAPS Passwords
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -u, --username USERNAME
                       username for LDAP
 -p, --password PASSWORD
                       password for LDAP (or LM:NT hash)
 -l, --ldapserver LDAPSERVER
                       LDAP server (or domain)
 -d, --domain DOMAIN   Domain
 -c, --computer COMPUTER
                       Target computer
 -o, --output OUTPUT   Output file to CSV

examples.command: lapsdumper: error: the following arguments are required: -u/--username, -p/--password, -d/--domain

binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Tool that dumps LAPS passwords
A tool that dumps every LAPS password the account
has the ability to read with a domain.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use lapsdumper because ### lapsdumper.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `lapsdumper [-h] -u USERNAME -p PASSWORD=$KFIOSA_SECRET LDAPSERVER] -d DOMAIN` as documented upstream for lapsdumper.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair lapsdumper with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `lapsdumper --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install lapsdumper. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `lapsdumper [-h] -u USERNAME -p PASSWORD=$KFIOSA_SECRET LDAPSERVER] -d DOMAIN`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### lapsdumper.
